perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, Buyer has designated Backhaul as a Buyer Designee for purposes of the Asset Purchase Agreement, and Buyer has assigned to Backhaul the right to receive the Purchased Assets pursuant to that certain Assignment of Asset Purchase Agreement between Buyer and Backhaul dated as of the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, Buyer has designated Backhaul as a Purchaser for purposes of the Asset Purchase Agreement, and Buyer has assigned to Backhaul the right to receive the Purchased Assets pursuant to that certain Assignment of Asset Purchase Agreement between Buyer and Backhaul dated as of the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Buyer Designee' is replaced with 'Purchaser'. While Backhaul's role remains as receiving the assets, the explicit designation is changed, creating ambiguity whether 'Purchaser' encompasses the same rights and obligations as initially intended for a 'Buyer Designee'. This contradiction causes uncertainty in interpreting the intended scope of Backhaul’s rights and responsibilities regarding the Purchased Assets, specifically if they are now seen as a simple 'Purchaser'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ASSIGNMENT: NOW, THEREFORE, for good and valuable consideration, the receipt and adequacy of which are hereby acknowledged, Seller does hereby transfer, sell, assign, convey and deliver to Backhaul all right, title and interest in, to and under the Assigned Intellectual Property, including, without limitation, the Trademarks and Patents set forth on Schedules A and B hereof, respectively, and all goodwill of the Purchased Business associated therewith.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Buyer Designee' to 'Purchaser' introduces ambiguity regarding Backhaul's specific rights and obligations as they relate to the Assigned Intellectual Property in the contradicted section. The original text clearly defines Backhaul as a 'Buyer Designee,' giving them certain assumed rights. The changed text, by simply designating them as 'Purchaser,' potentially alters the scope of their rights regarding the 'Assigned Intellectual Property' transfer from Seller, leading to uncertainty in interpreting Backhaul's intended responsibilities. This is an instance of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as it leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Seller hereby covenants and agrees, that from time to time forthwith upon the reasonable written request of Backhaul or Buyer, that Seller will, at Backhaul's cost and expense, do, execute, acknowledge and deliver or cause to be done, executed, acknowledged and delivered, each and all of such further acts, deeds, assignments, transfers, conveyances and assurances as may reasonably be required by Backhaul or Buyer in order to transfer, assign, convey and deliver unto and vest in Backhaul title to all right, title and interest of Seller in, to and under the Assigned Intellectual Property.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller hereby covenants and agrees, that from time to time forthwith upon the reasonable written request of Backhaul or Buyer, that Seller will, at Backhaul's expense, do, execute, acknowledge and deliver or cause to be done, executed, acknowledged and delivered, each and all of such further acts, deeds, assignments, transfers, conveyances and assurances as may reasonably be required by Backhaul or Buyer in order to transfer, assign, convey and deliver unto and vest in Backhaul title to all right, title and interest of Seller in, to and under the Assigned Intellectual Property.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'Backhaul's cost and expense' shifts responsibility of all expenses to Seller. Now 'Backhaul's cost and expense', which defined who should pay the costs associated to transfer, assign, convey and deliver unto and vest in Backhaul title to all right, title and interest of Seller in, to and under the Assigned Intellectual Property is ambiguous, leading to potential conflict during enforcement of the terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller hereby covenants and agrees, that from time to time forthwith upon the reasonable written request of Backhaul or Buyer, that Seller will, at Backhaul's expense, do, execute, acknowledge and deliver or cause to be done, executed, acknowledged and delivered, each and all of such further acts, deeds, assignments, transfers, conveyances and assurances as may reasonably be required by Backhaul or Buyer in order to transfer, assign, convey and deliver unto and vest in Backhaul title to all right, title and interest of Seller in, to and under the Assigned Intellectual Property.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states 'at Backhaul's cost and expense', indicating Backhaul is responsible for the costs. The changed text removes 'Backhaul's cost and expense', shifting the cost burden to the Seller. The contradicted text then states 'at Backhaul's expense'. The changed text contradicts the original text, which stated who should pay, creating a direct conflict over who is responsible for the expenses related to transferring the Assigned Intellectual Property.

perturbation.original_text: This Intellectual Property Agreement is subject in all respects to the terms and conditions of the Asset Purchase Agreement and is intended only to document the assignment of the Assigned Intellectual Property.
perturbation.changed_text: This Intellectual Property Agreement is partially subject to the terms and conditions of the Asset Purchase Agreement and is intended only to document the assignment of Intellectual Property.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'in all respects' to 'partially subject' introduces ambiguity as to which aspects of the Intellectual Property Agreement are governed by the Asset Purchase Agreement. Also, the removed of the word 'Assigned' implies there could be other types of Intellectual Property. This change creates uncertainty about the relationship between the two agreements and could lead to disputes over which terms take precedence, as it is not clear which terms are applicable. The 'Assigned' word is not used, creating a 'broader Intellectual Property'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing contained in this Intellectual Property Agreement shall be deemed to supersede any of the obligations, agreements, representations, covenants or warranties of Seller and Buyer contained in the Asset Purchase Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Intellectual Property Agreement is 'subject in all respects' to the Asset Purchase Agreement, providing a clear hierarchy. The changed text weakens this to 'partially subject', creating ambiguity. This directly contradicts the section stating 'Nothing contained in this Intellectual Property Agreement shall be deemed to supersede any of the obligations...contained in the Asset Purchase Agreement' because it's now unclear which aspects of the Intellectual Property Agreement are subordinate, potentially superseding parts of the Asset Purchase Agreement. The removal of the word 'Assigned' causes a broader Intellectual Property consideration.
